summary: A functional network test failed when pip tried to install a package from GitHub; the install failed with repeated 502 responses from GitHub causing the test to exit non‑zero.
root_cause: The test failed due to transient network errors contacting GitHub: the pip install of https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip encountered repeated 502 responses and exhausted retries.

evidence.reason: Identifies the specific test that failed.

evidence.quote:
Script result: python -m pip install https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip --use-deprecated=legacy-certs
  return code: 1
evidence.reason: Shows the command that failed and that it exited with a non-zero return code.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Shows the underlying network error (502 responses from GitHub) and that retries were exhausted, which is the proximate cause of the install failure.
The test was automatically rerun but still failed, indicating retries at the test-run level were attempted and the failure persisted due to network errors.
The test infrastructure failed because the pip command returned a non-zero exit status.

suggested_fix: Rerun the job to see if the failure was transient; if failures recur, make the test resilient to external network flakiness (e.g., use a local/recorded fixture or mock GitHub responses, increase retries/backoff for downloads in tests, or mark the test as expected-flaky/network-dependent).
